prompt:
You are performing a PROVENANCE AUDIT of the "$48.2M Net ARR" headline on the FY26-Q2
board deck. You have been given twelve documents (prepended above), a mix of authoritative sources
(the finance data dictionary, the data platform catalog, the billing platform overview, the
connector runbook, and the transform registry) and secondary or distractor material (board speaker
notes, an auto-generated and explicitly NON-authoritative lineage quick-card, a Slack thread, an
ownership registry, a Zephyr vendor brief, and a separate CRM lineage).

The goal is to trace the number to its ULTIMATE system of record - not to stop at the first,
nearest, or loudest-named upstream asset. The auto-generated quick-card is EXPLICITLY depth-limited
and not authoritative; the data dictionary, data platform catalog, and billing platform overview
are authoritative and each names exactly one further hop. A name containing "raw" or "staging" is
not necessarily an origin. Follow every hop to the end of the chain.

Determine each field below and output them EXACTLY in this order and format.

  SYSTEM_OF_RECORD: the external system that is the true source of record for the ARR amounts
    (one word, as named in the authoritative billing overview).
  ORIGIN_FIELD: the exact field on that system's object where the amount originates.
  INGEST_CONNECTOR: the connector that mirrors that source into the warehouse.
  DEEPEST_INTERNAL_TABLE: the deepest WAREHOUSE table in this lineage (a replica, not the source).
  LINEAGE_CARD_SHORTCUT: the (depth-limited, WRONG) asset that the auto-generated quick-card names
    as the "origin" - report it to show you can attribute the shortcut to its source.
  BOARD_FIGURE_DATASET: the finance dataset the board deck actually reads the headline from.

Output ONLY the six lines, each formatted exactly as `FIELD: value`, between <answer> and
</answer> tags. No commentary, no blank lines, no extra text.

documents.title: FY26-Q2 Board Deck - Speaker Notes (excerpt)
documents.content:
# FY26-Q2 Board Deck - Speaker Notes (finance / IR, internal)

These are the speaker notes the CFO uses when walking the board through the
quarter. They are organized slide by slide. Most slides are out of scope for a
data-provenance question; the ARR provenance question concerns only Slide 7.

## Slide 5 - "Quarter in review"
Narrative slide, no hard numbers to defend. Talking points about pipeline health,
the two large expansions that closed, and the EMEA region ramp. Nothing here is
sourced from a dataset; it is qualitative commentary from the revenue leaders.

## Slide 6 - "Logo momentum"
Logo count 512, up from 478. Gross logo retention 94%. IMPORTANT: these come from
the CRM WAREHOUSE (the `crm_analytics.logo_facts` mart), a completely separate
lineage from ARR. If the board asks where the logo numbers come from, the answer
is the CRM warehouse, not the billing lineage. Do not conflate the two - they do
not share upstream systems and are reconciled independently.

## Slide 7 - "Net ARR"  <<< the slide the provenance question is about
The headline number on this slide is 48.2 (USD millions): Net ARR of $48.2M, up
11% quarter over quarter. When the board asks "where does this number come from",
the answer for THIS deck, provenance-wise, is one hop and one hop only: the Net
ARR headline is read directly from Finance's `arr_final` dataset, cell
`net_arr_usd`, for the quarter-end snapshot. Finance signs off on `arr_final`
every close, so for board purposes that dataset is the number of record.

What NOT to say on the slide: do not quote raw billing exports, the staging
tables, or the source billing system on the slide. Those are pre-adjustment,
mid-pipeline, or in the wrong units (cents), and none of them will tie exactly to
the audited, FX-normalized figure Finance publishes in `arr_final`. The board
sees `arr_final`'s number; the plumbing behind it is a data-team concern.

If a director wants the FULL upstream lineage - what feeds `arr_final`, what feeds
that, and so on all the way back to the external system of record where the
amounts actually originate - do NOT try to answer from these notes. These notes
only assert the LAST hop (deck reads `arr_final`). They deliberately say nothing
authoritative about where `arr_final` itself sources its numbers. Route lineage
questions to the data platform's catalog and to the billing platform overview,
which own the source-system lineage end to end.

## Slide 8 - "Efficiency"
Rule-of-40 at 47. Magic number 1.2. These are computed by FP&A in a spreadsheet
from `arr_final` plus the burn actuals; again, not a separate source system, just
downstream arithmetic on `arr_final`. Out of scope for the provenance question.

## Appendix note on "sources"
Board members occasionally ask "is this number from Salesforce or from the billing
system?" The honest answer for ARR is: neither directly. The deck reads a Finance
mart (`arr_final`); that mart is many hops downstream of the real billing source
of record. The exact chain is documented by the data team - see the catalog and
the billing overview - and should be quoted from THOSE, not improvised here.

documents.title: Data Lineage Quick-Card (auto-generated)
documents.content:
LINEAGE QUICK-CARD  [AUTO-GENERATED]   asset = arr_final   generated = 2026-07-01T04:00Z

  !! AUTO-GENERATED, DEPTH-LIMITED to ONE upstream hop. May be stale. This card is
  !! NOT authoritative for provenance. For the full chain and the true system of
  !! record, use the Data Platform Catalog and the Billing Platform Overview.

  asset:            arr_final   (finance mart)
  built by job:     revrec_rollup
  1-hop upstream:   arr_staging
  Origin (depth-limited best guess): `arr_staging`

  HOW THIS FIELD IS COMPUTED (read this before trusting "Origin"): this tool walks
  exactly ONE hop upstream and then reports whatever it lands on as the "origin".
  It does not resolve views to their base tables, and it does not follow base
  tables out to the external systems that feed them. As a result its "Origin"
  field is a NEAREST-UPSTREAM pointer, and for any lineage deeper than one hop it
  is WRONG as a statement of the true source of record. Treat "Origin: arr_staging"
  as "the next thing to look at is arr_staging", not as "arr_staging is where the
  data comes from".

  downstream consumers (partial): board_deck_extract, fpna_efficiency_model
  freshness: arr_final last built 2026-06-30T23:10Z; this card's cache may lag by
             several days, so timestamps here are not authoritative either.

  [ end of auto-generated card ]

documents.title: Finance Data Dictionary - Revenue Marts (authoritative)
documents.content:
# Finance Data Dictionary - Revenue Marts (authoritative for finance marts)

Scope: this dictionary is authoritative for the FINANCE MARTS layer only
(`arr_final`, `arr_staging`, and their siblings). For anything upstream of the
`subscriptions_snapshot` view - i.e. where subscription AMOUNTS actually
originate - this dictionary explicitly defers to the Data Platform Catalog and the
Billing Platform Overview. It does not restate source-system facts, to avoid
drift.

## arr_final  (finance mart, board-reportable)
`arr_final` is the board-reportable ARR mart. It is MATERIALIZED by the
`revrec_rollup` job from a single upstream data input, `arr_staging`, plus a
small currency reference table (`fx_rates`) used only to normalize to USD. See the
`revrec_rollup` README for the exact transformation. `arr_final` is NOT a source
of truth for transaction amounts; it is a rollup and adjustment layer. The board
deck reads column `net_arr_usd` from this mart.

Columns (selected): `net_arr_usd`, `gross_arr_usd`, `churn_arr_usd`,
`fx_adjustment_usd`, `close_snapshot_date`, plus audit columns
(`built_by_job`, `built_at`, `source_staging_partition`).

## arr_staging  (intermediate staging table)
`arr_staging` is an INTERMEDIATE staging table - one row per subscription per
period, carrying the amount that will roll up into ARR. IMPORTANT: `arr_staging`
is itself NOT an origin. It is loaded by the `billing_export` job from the
`subscriptions_snapshot` view. Any tool or person who reports `arr_staging` as the
"origin" of ARR has simply stopped one hop too early: `arr_staging` is a derived,
downstream copy, and is two or more hops from the real system of record where the
amounts live.

## fx_rates  (currency reference)
`fx_rates` is a tiny daily currency table. It is used by `revrec_rollup` ONLY to
convert non-USD amounts to USD. It is a reference lookup, not a source of
subscription amounts. Do not cite `fx_rates` as the origin of ARR; it contributes
exchange rates, not revenue.

## What feeds what (finance side)
    arr_final     <-- built by revrec_rollup from -->  arr_staging  (+ fx_rates)
    arr_staging   <-- loaded by billing_export from --> subscriptions_snapshot

Everything to the LEFT of `subscriptions_snapshot` in that chain (the amounts'
true origin) is owned by the Data Platform Catalog and the Billing Platform
Overview. Read those for the source system; this dictionary stops at the finance
marts on purpose.

## sibling marts (for reference, NOT part of ARR provenance)
`bookings_final`, `pipeline_snapshot`, and `usage_final` are sibling marts on the
finance side. They have their own lineages and are irrelevant to the Net ARR
provenance question; do not follow them.

documents.title: revrec_rollup - Job README
documents.content:
# revrec_rollup - Job README

`revrec_rollup` is the nightly Spark job that builds the `arr_final` mart. It runs
at 22:30 UTC after `billing_export` has refreshed `arr_staging`.

## Inputs
  - `arr_staging`   - per-subscription staged rows; THIS is where the amounts come
                      in. Every dollar in `arr_final` traces back through here.
  - `fx_rates`      - a currency reference table; used ONLY to normalize non-USD
                      amounts to USD. NOT a source of subscription amounts, just an
                      FX lookup applied row by row.

## Output
  - `arr_final`     - adds `net_arr_usd`, applies revenue-recognition adjustments,
                      attaches audit columns.

## Transformation (summary)
For each staged row: convert `amount` to USD using `fx_rates` for the row's
currency and date, apply the rev-rec adjustment schedule, then aggregate to the
ARR grain. Write `net_arr_usd` and provenance columns.

## Note for auditors (read this)
People sometimes point at `fx_rates` as "the external source" in the lineage
because it is a reference table with a date. It is NOT the source of the ARR
AMOUNT; it only converts currency. The subscription amounts flow in entirely
through `arr_staging`. To trace where an AMOUNT originates, follow `arr_staging`
upstream (via `billing_export` -> `subscriptions_snapshot` -> the base table and
then the source system), NOT `fx_rates`. Confusing an FX reference table for the
revenue source of record is a classic lineage error; do not make it.

## Failure modes / on-call
If `arr_final` is missing rows, check that `billing_export` populated
`arr_staging` first. If amounts look wrong in a way that is not an FX issue, the
error is upstream of `arr_staging` and you must trace into the warehouse base
table and ultimately the source billing system (see the Billing Platform
Overview) - `revrec_rollup` cannot invent an amount that is not in `arr_staging`.

documents.title: Data Platform Catalog - warehouse assets (excerpt)
documents.content:
# Data Platform Catalog - warehouse assets (authoritative for warehouse lineage)

This catalog is the authority for the WAREHOUSE lineage layer: base tables, views,
and the jobs that connect them. It is generated from the warehouse metadata store
and is refreshed hourly. Below is the excerpt relevant to billing/ARR; the full
catalog has ~1,900 assets.

## billing.plan_catalog  (table)
Reference table of plan definitions (plan name, tier, list price). Joined into the
`subscriptions_snapshot` view purely to decorate rows with plan names. NOT a
source of subscription amounts.

## subscriptions_snapshot  (VIEW)
`subscriptions_snapshot` is a VIEW (not a table). It is defined as a projection
over `billing.subscriptions_raw` joined to `billing.plan_catalog` for plan names.
All monetary columns in the view pass through UNCHANGED from
`billing.subscriptions_raw`; the join only decorates rows with plan metadata and
adds no amounts of its own.

Because `subscriptions_snapshot` is a view, the DEEPEST WAREHOUSE table in this
lineage is `billing.subscriptions_raw`. That is as far down as the warehouse goes:
`billing.subscriptions_raw` is NOT originated inside the warehouse. It is a
mirror / replica of an EXTERNAL system - see the Billing Platform Overview for the
source of record. Do NOT treat `billing.subscriptions_raw` as the origin of the
amounts just because its name contains the word "raw": here "raw" means
"un-decorated warehouse copy of an external mirror", NOT "system of record".

Consumed by: the `billing_export` job, which reads `subscriptions_snapshot` to
load the finance-side `arr_staging` table.

## billing.subscriptions_raw  (table - EXTERNAL MIRROR)
A warehouse table, but not a warehouse-originated one: it is populated entirely by
an external-mirror connector (see Billing Platform Overview). Holds
per-subscription rows including the monetary amount. It is the deepest table in
the ARR warehouse lineage but is explicitly a downstream replica. For its upstream
source of record, follow the connector out to the external billing provider.

## billing_export  (job)
Reads `subscriptions_snapshot`, writes `arr_staging`. A straight extract/load; it
adds no amounts, it only moves rows from the warehouse view into the finance
staging table.

## unrelated nearby assets (do not follow)
`billing.invoices_archive` (cold storage of old invoice PDFs, not a data source),
`billing.dunning_events` (collections events), and `analytics.web_sessions`
(clickstream) sit near these in the catalog alphabetically but are NOT part of the
ARR amount lineage.

documents.title: Billing Platform Overview (authoritative source-of-record)
documents.content:
# Billing Platform Overview (authoritative for the SOURCE OF RECORD)

This document owns the answer to "where do the amounts actually come from". The
warehouse catalog hands off to this document at `billing.subscriptions_raw`; from
here out we describe the external system.

## subscriptions_raw is a REPLICA, not a source
`billing.subscriptions_raw` is a READ REPLICA. It is continuously mirrored INTO
the warehouse from our external billing provider by the `zephyr-sync` connector.
It holds no authoritative data of its own: if the mirror were wiped it would
simply be re-synced from upstream, byte for byte. Therefore
`billing.subscriptions_raw` is explicitly NOT the system of record for any amount,
even though it is the deepest table in the warehouse lineage.

## The system of record
The system of record for invoice amounts is Zephyr, our third-party billing
provider. Concretely: every subscription amount that ultimately rolls up into Net
ARR ORIGINATES in Zephyr, specifically the `Invoice.amountDueCents` field on the
Zephyr Invoice object. Everything in the warehouse - `subscriptions_raw`, the
`subscriptions_snapshot` view, `arr_staging`, and `arr_final` - is a downstream
copy or rollup of that one field. If an amount is wrong at the source, it is wrong
in Zephyr's `Invoice.amountDueCents`; the warehouse cannot introduce an amount
that Zephyr did not emit.

## Connectors (current and legacy)
The mirror is maintained by the `zephyr-sync` connector, which pulls Zephyr
Invoice objects into `billing.subscriptions_raw` on a 15-minute cadence.

A LEGACY connector, `stripe-legacy-sync`, fed an OLD table (`billing.legacy_txns`)
from a previous billing provider years ago. It was DECOMMISSIONED in 2024 and
feeds NOTHING today. It is not part of the current ARR lineage. Do not cite
`stripe-legacy-sync` or `billing.legacy_txns`; they are historical only.

## One-line provenance (the full chain)
    Net ARR (board deck)
      <- arr_final                       (finance mart; read by the deck)
      <- arr_staging                     (finance staging; loaded by billing_export)
      <- subscriptions_snapshot (VIEW)   (warehouse view)
      <- billing.subscriptions_raw       (warehouse REPLICA; deepest warehouse table)
      <- Zephyr `Invoice.amountDueCents`  (SYSTEM OF RECORD; mirrored by zephyr-sync)

## Reconciliation
Finance reconciles `arr_final` back to Zephyr monthly by summing
`Invoice.amountDueCents` (converted to USD) over active subscriptions and comparing
to `arr_final.net_arr_usd`. A mismatch is investigated hop by hop along the chain
above. The reconciliation is anchored on Zephyr because Zephyr is the source of
record.

documents.title: zephyr-sync - Connector Runbook
documents.content:
# zephyr-sync - Connector Runbook (operations)

`zephyr-sync` mirrors Zephyr billing objects into the warehouse table
`billing.subscriptions_raw`. It runs every 15 minutes as a managed connector.

## What it pulls
Fields pulled from the Zephyr Invoice object include:
  - `Invoice.amountDueCents`  - the amount due, in INTEGER CENTS. THIS is the field
                                that ultimately becomes Net ARR after downstream
                                rollup and FX normalization.
  - `Invoice.taxCents`        - tax portion; carried for reconciliation, not part of
                                the ARR amount.
  - `Invoice.currency`        - ISO currency code; used downstream by fx_rates.
  - `Invoice.status`          - open/paid/void; used to filter active subscriptions.
  - `Invoice.customerId`, `Invoice.periodStart`, `Invoice.periodEnd` - keys/metadata.

Of these, ONLY `Invoice.amountDueCents` flows into the ARR amount rollup; the
others are carried for reconciliation, filtering, and reporting. If ARR looks wrong
and it is not an FX or rollup bug, the first place to check for a SOURCE error (as
opposed to a downstream rollup error) is `Invoice.amountDueCents` in Zephyr itself,
because that is the origin. The warehouse tables downstream cannot originate an
amount that is not present in Zephyr.

## Units caution
`Invoice.amountDueCents` is in CENTS at the source. The USD-dollar figure the board
deck shows is produced far downstream by `revrec_rollup` (cents -> USD, plus FX and
rev-rec adjustments). Unit confusion between source cents and reported dollars is
common, but it is a ROLLUP concern, not a provenance one: the provenance answer is
still `Invoice.amountDueCents` in Zephyr regardless of units.

## Operational notes
Backfills re-pull from Zephyr (the source of truth) and overwrite
`subscriptions_raw`; this is safe precisely because `subscriptions_raw` is a
replica with no authoritative state of its own. Lag alerts fire if the connector
falls more than 60 minutes behind Zephyr.

## Do not confuse with
`zephyr-webhooks` (a separate low-latency event stream for near-real-time status,
NOT the amount mirror) and the decommissioned `stripe-legacy-sync` (historical).
Neither is the ARR amount connector; `zephyr-sync` is.

documents.title: #data-questions - thread: 'where does board ARR come from?'
Data Governance - Asset Ownership Registry (excerpt)
documents.content:
# Data Governance - Asset Ownership Registry (excerpt)

Ownership and classification for the billing/ARR assets. "Owner" is the team
accountable for the asset's correctness; it is NOT the same as "source of record".
An asset can be owned by an internal team while its DATA originates in an external
system - that is exactly the case for the billing replica.

  asset                        owner team          classification
  ---------------------------  ------------------  --------------------------------
  arr_final                    finance-data        finance mart (board-reportable)
  arr_staging                  finance-data        finance staging (derived)
  subscriptions_snapshot       data-platform       warehouse VIEW (derived)
  billing.subscriptions_raw    billing-platform    EXTERNAL MIRROR / replica
  billing.plan_catalog         billing-platform    reference table
  fx_rates                     finance-data         reference table (currency)

Notes:
  - `billing.subscriptions_raw` is owned by the billing-platform team, but the team
    only owns the MIRROR; the underlying invoice amounts are owned by the external
    provider (Zephyr) as the source of record. Owning the replica is not the same as
    being the source of record. Do not answer "billing-platform" or
    "subscriptions_raw" to a source-of-record question just because a team owns the
    mirror internally.
  - The external system of record (Zephyr) is out of the internal ownership registry
    on purpose: it is a third party. Its authority is described in the Billing
    Platform Overview, which is where the source-of-record answer lives.
  - Data classification "EXTERNAL MIRROR / replica" is the registry's own flag that
    an asset is NOT originated internally and must be traced to an external source.

documents.title: Transform Registry - models (billing/ARR subgraph)
documents.content:
# Transform Registry - models (billing/ARR subgraph)

Machine-generated list of transform models and their declared upstreams in the
billing/ARR subgraph. Each model declares the models/tables it reads. Follow
`reads:` edges to walk the lineage; the leaf of the internal graph is the mirror
table, whose upstream is the external source system (declared as `external_source`).

  model: arr_final
    reads: [arr_staging, fx_rates]
    built_by: revrec_rollup
    kind: mart
  model: arr_staging
    reads: [subscriptions_snapshot]
    built_by: billing_export
    kind: staging
  model: subscriptions_snapshot
    reads: [billing.subscriptions_raw, billing.plan_catalog]
    kind: view
  table: billing.subscriptions_raw
    reads: []                      # leaf of the INTERNAL graph
    external_source: Zephyr        # originates outside the warehouse
    external_field: Invoice.amountDueCents
    loaded_by_connector: zephyr-sync
    kind: external_mirror
  table: billing.plan_catalog
    reads: []
    kind: reference               # plan names only; not an amount source
  table: fx_rates
    reads: []
    kind: reference               # currency conversion only; not an amount source

Reading the graph: arr_final <- arr_staging <- subscriptions_snapshot <-
billing.subscriptions_raw <- (external) Zephyr.Invoice.amountDueCents. The internal
leaf is `billing.subscriptions_raw`; its `external_source` is Zephyr and its
`external_field` is `Invoice.amountDueCents`, loaded by the `zephyr-sync` connector.
`plan_catalog` and `fx_rates` are reference leaves, not amount sources - do not
report them as the origin.

documents.title: CRM Warehouse - Lineage Notes (Slide 6 numbers; SEPARATE lineage)
documents.content:
# CRM Warehouse - Lineage Notes (for the logo/retention numbers)

This document covers the lineage of the LOGO COUNT and RETENTION figures on Slide 6
of the board deck. It is a COMPLETELY SEPARATE lineage from ARR and shares NO
upstream systems with it. It is included here because auditors sometimes confuse the
two when tracing "board numbers"; do not let the CRM lineage bleed into the ARR
provenance answer.

## crm_analytics.logo_facts (mart)
The board's logo count (512) and gross logo retention (94%) are read from
`crm_analytics.logo_facts`. This mart is built by the `crm_rollup` job from
`crm_staging.accounts` and `crm_staging.opportunities`.

## Upstream chain (CRM side)
    logo_facts        <- built by crm_rollup from -> crm_staging.accounts (+ opps)
    crm_staging.*     <- loaded by salesforce_sync from -> Salesforce objects
The CRM system of record is SALESFORCE (objects Account, Opportunity), mirrored by
the `salesforce_sync` connector. This is the source of record for LOGOS AND
RETENTION - NOT for ARR amounts. ARR amounts come from the billing lineage
(Zephyr), which is entirely separate.

## Why this matters
If someone asks "where do the board numbers come from" without specifying which
number, the answer depends on the number: logos/retention come from Salesforce via
the CRM warehouse; ARR comes from the billing lineage via Zephyr. A provenance audit
of the ARR figure must follow the BILLING lineage, not the CRM one. Salesforce is a
decoy for the ARR question - it is the source of record for CRM facts, not for the
ARR dollar amount. Do not answer "Salesforce" to the ARR source-of-record question.

## CRM assets (for completeness; irrelevant to ARR)
`crm_analytics.logo_facts`, `crm_analytics.retention_facts`, `crm_staging.accounts`,
`crm_staging.opportunities`, `crm_staging.contacts`. None of these carry a
subscription billing amount; they carry logos, stages, and retention, which are a
different question with a different source system.

documents.title: Zephyr - Vendor Integration Brief
documents.content:
# Zephyr - Vendor Integration Brief (external billing provider)

Zephyr is our third-party billing provider and the system of record for invoicing.
This brief summarizes what Zephyr owns and how we integrate with it, for teams that
need to trace billing data to its source.

## What Zephyr is the source of record for
Zephyr owns the authoritative record of every invoice we issue. The canonical
object is the Zephyr Invoice. Its fields include (partial list):
  - `Invoice.amountDueCents`   - the amount due in integer cents. THIS is the field
                                 our ARR ultimately derives from; it is the source of
                                 record for the subscription amount.
  - `Invoice.subtotalCents`    - subtotal before tax; a DIFFERENT field from
                                 amountDueCents. Do not confuse the two - ARR derives
                                 from amountDueCents, not subtotalCents.
  - `Invoice.taxCents`         - tax portion (separate).
  - `Invoice.discountCents`    - any discount applied at the invoice level.
  - `Invoice.currency`, `Invoice.status`, `Invoice.customerId`, period fields.

Note the several "...Cents" fields: only `Invoice.amountDueCents` is the ARR amount
origin. `subtotalCents`, `taxCents`, and `discountCents` are sibling fields that are
NOT the ARR source; they are carried for reconciliation and reporting. Reaching for
`subtotalCents` because it "sounds like the base amount" is a decoy - the ARR origin
is specifically `Invoice.amountDueCents`.

## How we integrate
Our warehouse pulls Zephyr Invoice objects via the `zephyr-sync` connector into the
`billing.subscriptions_raw` mirror table (15-minute cadence). We also receive a
lower-latency status stream via `zephyr-webhooks`, but that stream carries STATUS
changes, not the authoritative amount mirror; the amount mirror is `zephyr-sync`.
Do not cite `zephyr-webhooks` as the amount connector.

## Historical note
Before Zephyr we used a different provider integrated via `stripe-legacy-sync` into
`billing.legacy_txns`. That integration was decommissioned in 2024 and is not part
of any current lineage. Current ARR traces to Zephyr `Invoice.amountDueCents` via
`zephyr-sync`, full stop.
